mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase corporate borrowing costs and compress equity valuation multiples, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean excess returns when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and recession risk, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to separate bull and bear regimes with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate earnings growth and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both exceed their 12-month moving averages
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative mean returns over 60 days in > 60% of occurrences, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which drives positive equity returns over 20 trading days when inflation is contained.
failure_condition: Signal generates negative returns during low-inflation periods, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio falls below 0.5 over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of restrictive federal funds rates and persistently high consumer price inflation creates stagflationary pressure that compresses profit margins and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are positive in > 50% of triggered cases, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for market volatility.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, which supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC remains below 0.01, or 60-day forward returns show no statistical difference between high and low payroll growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: A quarter-over-quarter decline in real gross domestic product confirms economic contraction, prompting risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in > 65% of contraction quarters, or the signal fails to predict direction with accuracy exceeding 55% in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals strengthening manufacturing demand and capacity utilization, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days when monetary policy remains stable.
failure_condition: Signal produces negative mean returns during stable policy periods, or out-of-sample directional accuracy drops below 50% over consecutive 12-month windows.
